skill.name: context-compression
skill.description: Design and evaluate structured compression strategies for long-running AI agent sessions.
skill.summary: Helps teams preserve task-critical context while reducing long AI session histories.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported markdown backticks, a GitHub source URL, weak-crypto keywords, and reconnaissance terms. Manual review found these are documentation false positives in SKILL.md, with no executable code, command invocation, credential handling, network call, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

content.user_title: Improve Context Compression for Long Sessions
content.value_statement: Long AI sessions lose important details when history is compressed poorly. This skill provides structured methods to preserve decisions, files, errors, and next steps.
actual_capabilities: Explains anchored iterative summarization, opaque compression, and regenerative summary approaches.
Defines structured summary sections for intent, files, decisions, current state, and next steps.
Recommends compression trigger strategies such as fixed thresholds, sliding windows, and task boundaries.
Describes probe-based evaluation for recall, artifact tracking, continuation, and decision quality.
Provides practical guidance for multi-phase compression in large codebase work.
limitations: Provides methodology and examples, not an executable compression engine.
Does not include the referenced evaluation-framework file in the scanned file structure.
Compression quality still depends on the model and summary prompt used by the agent.
Does not automatically track files or validate summaries against a repository.

use_cases.title: Preserve Coding Session State
use_cases.description: Use structured summaries to keep modified files, decisions, failing tests, and next steps available after context compaction.

use_cases.title: Design Agent Memory Policies
use_cases.description: Compare compression triggers and summary formats before building memory behavior into long-running agent workflows.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Summary Quality
use_cases.description: Create probes that test whether compressed context preserves facts, artifacts, decisions, and continuation plans.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Summary Template
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a long AI session
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a structured context summary template for my agent session. Include sections for goal, files changed, decisions, current state, risks, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Compression Trigger
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate selection of when to compact context
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare fixed threshold, sliding window, importance-based, and task-boundary compression for my workflow. Recommend one trigger strategy and explain the trade-offs.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Compression Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this compressed session summary for missing artifacts, vague decisions, lost error details, and weak next steps. Suggest precise improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Probe Evaluation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a probe-based evaluation plan for context compression. Include recall, artifact, continuation, decision, and instruction-following probes with scoring criteria.

output_examples.input: A debugging session is near the context limit after tracing an authentication failure across several files.
output_examples.output: A structured summary that records the login error, root cause, files read, files changed, test status, decisions, and remaining fixes.

output_examples.input: A team wants to know whether its compression policy loses important implementation details.
output_examples.output: A probe set that asks targeted questions about file paths, error messages, decisions, current blockers, and next actions.

output_examples.input: An agent platform needs a default compaction policy for long codebase exploration.
output_examples.output: A recommendation to use anchored iterative summaries with a sliding window and separate artifact tracking for file state.

best_practices: Optimize for total tokens per completed task, not only tokens per request.
Use explicit summary sections so critical details have a required place to appear.
Test compressed summaries with probes before relying on them in production workflows.
anti_patterns: Compressing aggressively without checking whether file paths and decisions remain recoverable.
Regenerating the full summary every time when incremental updates would preserve history better.
Treating high lexical similarity as proof that the compressed context is useful.

faq.question: Does this skill execute compression automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, templates, evaluation ideas, and decision criteria for designing compression workflows.

faq.question: Which compression method does it prefer?
faq.answer: It favors anchored iterative summarization when file tracking, decisions, and task continuity matter.

faq.question: Can it help with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code sessions?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance applies to long AI agent sessions across Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What problem does probe-based evaluation solve?
faq.answer: It checks whether compressed context still answers practical questions needed to continue the task.

faq.question: Is opaque compression always worse?
faq.answer: No. It can save more tokens, but it is harder to inspect and may lose details needed for coding tasks.

faq.question: Does this skill include security-sensitive behavior?
faq.answer: No executable behavior was found in the reviewed file. The content is documentation about compression strategy.
